decision.overall_recommendation: VIX gate is open, so new entries are permissible today. BB is the standout (SmartScore 97, 5.29 R:R, Double Bottom already confirmed breaking out, mildly positive ML edge) but is now 87.5% above its volume-profile POC, meaning support underneath is thin. BTDR and CIFR both score well but carry conflicting independent signals: BTDR's technical setup and pattern are constructive yet the model's own 5-day edge call is negative, while CIFR has the best R:R (9.2) and a positive ML edge but its highest-confidence pattern is a Double Top with an explicit 'avoid long' action — a real contradiction worth respecting even with SmartScore already penalizing it. CORZ repeats the Double Top warning (already breaking down, 100% confidence) plus below-average volume and bearish Bitcoin-market headlines, making it the weakest of the crypto-infrastructure names. AVTR is the lowest-conviction pick overall: lowest SmartScore, negative ML edge, price extended above POC, and mixed daily/weekly trend alignment. ML track record has insufficient sample size this run, so the negative_ml_edge flags on BTDR and AVTR are taken at face value rather than adjusted for model reliability; pick track record also lacks sufficient data for a conviction calibration note. None of these tickers overlap with existing open orders (BNO, NEXT stop-losses), and current Webull holdings are sector-unclassified so no direct portfolio sector-concentration conflict is detectable — but note 3 of the 5 shortlisted names (BB, BTDR, CORZ) are all Technology, so avoid stacking multiple of these simultaneously. Position sizes are necessarily small given the $1,710.83 account balance and 1% risk-per-trade cap.
